children.text: Steel industry layoffs devastated the tax base in small Mahoning Valley cities like Campbell. According to a 1983 Wall Street Journal piece, the nearby small city of Struthers closed its parks and swimming pool, and laid off ten full-time municipal employees. According to John Kovach, the city finance director, “we derive the majority of our income from the city income tax. And 50 percent of our workers aren’t getting income.”

The economy continued to struggle in Campbell, as efforts to revive the local mill failed and deindustrialization in the region continued. According to Census and Bureau of Labor Statistics data, the population of Mahoning County fell by around 13% between 1970 and 1990. Over the same time period, the poverty rate increased from 9.2% to 15.9%.
Indeed, LTV Steel did end up buying the parent company of Youngstown Sheet and Tube. LTV filed for bankruptcy in 1986, and finally dissolved in 2001.
